title: acetosella
intro: Acetosella, also known as the cranberry-vine or mountain cranberry, is a low-growing, woody vine native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant contains malic acid and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable medicinal resource. The health benefits of Acetosella include its ability to support urinary tract health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Acetosella can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a variety of conditions.
